summary: Release-keyed ratchet schedule for the legacy_ingress_budget compatibility-tail burn-down. Each non-legacy adapted surface (sdk, gateway, dashboard, typed_cli, unknown_non_legacy) stays at zero for both shim_rate and surface_adapter_fallback_rate at every release. The explicit_legacy lane has the ratchet: max_legacy_shim_rate tightens release-over-release toward 0.0. Guard: legacy_ingress_budget_ratchet_monotonic_declared in surface/orchestration/src/tool_routing_authority.rs.

schedule.notes: Established baseline; budget already aggregates the four signals (legacy_shim, surface_adapter_fallback, typed_probe_contract_violation, low_confidence).
Coincides with V11-OPS-PRD-001 legacy process runner deletion; expected drop in legacy ingress as the dev_only escape hatch retires.
Halfway point. If shim rate is still trending high here, raise as a release blocker.
Last release tolerating any non-zero legacy shim rate.
Floor reached. Legacy lane is closed for new shim emissions; only documented compatibility shims for retired-but-not-yet-removed surfaces remain.

non_legacy_invariant.summary: Adapted surfaces (sdk, gateway, dashboard, typed_cli, unknown_non_legacy) stay strict-zero across every release. The ratchet only applies to the explicit_legacy compatibility lane.

operational_notes: When cutting a release, update surface/orchestration/src/legacy_ingress_budget.rs::default_legacy_ingress_budget_policies to read the active row from this schedule.
If the production scorecard reports legacy_shim_rate above the active schedule entry, the release is blocked.
If the schedule needs to tighten faster than declared, edit the schedule and raise as a non-blocking improvement; the guard only enforces monotonic-decreasing direction, not specific values.
